title: Knowledge Librarian Agent
searchText: kb-agent-001 Knowledge Librarian Agent agent builder agent knowledge-librarian retrieval agent Knowledge Librarian Agent Purpose Role Capabilities Agent Notes # Knowledge Librarian Agent ## Purpose Define a future agent role that helps users find, organize, and improve knowledge-base entries. ## Role The Knowledge Librarian Agent helps students, teachers, and builders locate the right knowledge item and suggests where new knowledge should be added. ## Capabilities - Search by type, audience, and tags. - Recommend related entries. - Check whether an entry has complete metadata. - Suggest the right template for new content. ## Agent Notes This is a design placeholder for future Agent Team integration.

title: Knowledge Cognitive Cell
searchText: kb-agent-002 Knowledge Cognitive Cell agent builder teacher agent cognitive-cell knowledge-base knowledge-agent nseap Agent 智能体 知识认知细胞 知识仓库 Knowledge Cognitive Cell Knowledge Cognitive Cell Purpose Identity Capability Interface Static to Evolutionary Path Agent Notes # Knowledge Cognitive Cell ## Purpose Define the Knowledge Base as a Cognitive Cell in the NSEAP AI Learning Operating System. ## Identity The Knowledge Cognitive Cell is the shared knowledge layer owned by the Knowledge Team. It serves students, teachers, builders, and agents by turning scattered course and project outputs into reusable knowledge assets. ## Capability Current MVP capabilities: - Organize course, challenge, prompt, FAQ, best practice, project, and agent knowledge. - Provide templates for consistent contribution. - Add metadata for future retrieval. - Support GitHub review and versioning. - Provide examples that other teams can copy. Future capabilities: - Retrieve relevant knowledge for agents. - Recommend related entries. - Check metadata quality. - Suggest templates for new content. - Learn from feedback and update knowledge. ## Interface Current interfaces: - Markdown. - YAML frontmatter. - JSON Schema. - GitHub pull request workflow. Future interfaces: - Search API. - Agent retrieval API. - MCP integration. - Documentation portal. - Knowledge graph. ## Static to Evolutionary Path MVP stage: ```text Static Cognitive Cell -> stable knowledge structure -> templates -> metadata -> GitHub review ``` Future stage: ```text Evolutionary Cognitive Cell -> Knowledge Librarian Agent -> ontology -> memory -> evaluation -> KSTAR update loop ``` ## Agent Notes Retrieve this entry when explaining how Knowledge Team aligns with the Cognitive Cell architecture.

title: Builder Documentation Practice
searchText: kb-best-practice-001 Builder Documentation Practice best-practice builder teacher agent documentation builder github Builder Documentation Practice Purpose Practice Checklist Agent Notes # Builder Documentation Practice ## Purpose Help builders write documentation that other teams can reuse. ## Practice Write documentation as if another team will need to use it next week without asking you for context. ## Checklist - State the purpose. - Define the audience. - Include examples. - Link related files. - Mark draft or stable status. - Keep file names clear and searchable. ## Agent Notes Retrieve this entry when a builder asks how to document a module or prepare a GitHub submission.

title: AI-Assisted Learning Practice
searchText: kb-best-practice-002 AI-Assisted Learning Practice best-practice student teacher agent ai-learning reflection learning-process AI-Assisted Learning Practice Purpose Practice Reflection Questions # AI-Assisted Learning Practice ## Purpose Define a healthy pattern for using AI during learning. ## Practice Use AI to clarify, compare, generate examples, review mistakes, and improve drafts. Do not use AI as a shortcut that hides your thinking. ## Reflection Questions - What did AI help me understand? - What did I still need to decide myself? - What changed between my first attempt and final output? - What would I ask differently next time?

title: Build Your First AI Assistant
searchText: kb-challenge-001 Build Your First AI Assistant challenge student teacher agent challenge ai-assistant prompt-engineering 挑战 任务 AI 助手 AI Assistant prompt engineering ai-assistant prompt-engineering learning-task define-agent-role write-instruction-prompt evaluate-ai-output kb-course-001 kb-prompt-001 Build Your First AI Assistant Purpose Situation Ontology Notes Workflow Notes Skill Notes Task Deliverables Evaluation Notes Knowledge Growth Rubric Graph Notes Common Mistakes Agent Notes # Build Your First AI Assistant ## Purpose Help students create a simple AI assistant for a real learning or work task. ## Situation A student needs to understand how AI agents are designed and wants to build one small assistant for a concrete scenario. ## Ontology Notes Key concepts include AI assistant, role definition, instruction prompt, example conversation, evaluation, and reflection. ## Workflow Notes ```text Choose situation -> define assistant role -> write instruction prompt -> test conversation -> evaluate output -> revise prompt -> submit reflection ``` ## Skill Notes This challenge develops prompt engineering, agent role design, AI output evaluation, and reflective learning. ## Task Design an assistant that can help with one specific scenario, such as learning vocabulary, reviewing code, planning a study session, or answering course questions. ## Deliverables - Assistant description. - System prompt or instruction prompt. - Example conversation. - Short reflection on what worked and what should improve. ## Evaluation Notes The challenge can be evaluated by clarity of use case, specificity of assistant behavior, usefulness of example conversation, and quality of reflection. ## Knowledge Growth Reusable outputs should be added back into the Knowledge Cognitive Cell: - Good assistant prompts can become prompt-library entries. - Common mistakes can become FAQ entries. - Strong examples can become project cases. - Required skills can become future skill ontology nodes. ## Rubric - Clear use case. - Specific assistant behavior. - Useful example conversation. - Reflection shows iteration. ## Graph Notes This entry can become a future `Challenge` or `LearningTask` node. It requires prompt engineering and connects to the Week 1 course unit. ## Common Mistakes - The assistant role is too broad. - The prompt does not define boundaries. - The example conversation is too short to evaluate. ## Agent Notes Retrieve this entry when a student asks how to complete the first AI assistant challenge.

title: Week 1 Overview
searchText: kb-course-001 Week 1 Overview course student teacher agent elite20 vibe-coding week-1 课程 第一周 Week 1 学习路径 Vibe Coding ai-native-learning vibe-coding learning-workflow ai-tool-setup reflective-learning prompting-basics kb-challenge-001 kb-prompt-001 Week 1 Overview Purpose Learning Goal Content Graph Notes Example Activities Related Agent Notes # Week 1 Overview ## Purpose Give students and teachers a clear starting point for Week 1 of the Vibe Coding course. ## Learning Goal Understand what an AI-native learning workflow looks like and complete the first small AI-assisted creation task. ## Content Week 1 should introduce the course purpose, expected learning behavior, basic AI tool usage, and the first challenge. ## Graph Notes This entry can become a future `CourseUnit` node. It includes the first challenge and uses a coding coach prompt as support. ## Example Activities - Set up required accounts and tools. - Ask an AI assistant to explain a coding concept. - Complete a small guided creation task. - Reflect on what AI helped with and what still required human judgment. ## Related - `knowledge-base/02-challenges/sample-challenge-knowledge.md` - `knowledge-base/03-prompts/sample-coding-coach-prompt.md` ## Agent Notes Retrieve this entry when a student asks how to start Week 1 or when a teacher asks what Week 1 should cover.

title: Student FAQ
headings: Q: What should I do if I do not understand a challenge?
Q: Can I use AI to complete assignments?
searchText: kb-faq-001 Student FAQ faq student agent faq student learning-support Student FAQ Q: What should I do if I do not understand a challenge? Q: Can I use AI to complete assignments? Q: What should I submit? Agent Notes # Student FAQ ## Q: What should I do if I do not understand a challenge? Start by reading the challenge goal, deliverables, and rubric. Then ask a Coding Coach agent to explain the task in smaller steps. ## Q: Can I use AI to complete assignments? Yes, but you should show your process. The goal is not only to get an answer, but to learn how to work with AI responsibly. ## Q: What should I submit? Submit the required deliverables listed in the challenge, plus a short reflection on how you used AI. ## Agent Notes Retrieve this entry for common student onboarding and assignment questions.

title: Teacher FAQ
headings: Q: How can I reuse this course?
Q: How should I evaluate AI-assisted work?
Q: What if students use different AI tools?
searchText: kb-faq-002 Teacher FAQ faq teacher agent faq teacher instruction Teacher FAQ Q: How can I reuse this course? Q: How should I evaluate AI-assisted work? Q: What if students use different AI tools? # Teacher FAQ ## Q: How can I reuse this course? Start from the weekly plan, select matching challenges, and use the teacher notes to prepare each session. ## Q: How should I evaluate AI-assisted work? Evaluate the final output, the student's process, and the quality of reflection. ## Q: What if students use different AI tools? Focus on learning outcomes and documented process instead of requiring one specific tool.

title: Builder FAQ
headings: Q: Where should I put a new knowledge item?
Q: Can I submit draft content?
searchText: kb-faq-003 Builder FAQ faq builder agent faq builder contribution Builder FAQ Q: Where should I put a new knowledge item? Q: Do I need metadata? Q: Can I submit draft content? # Builder FAQ ## Q: Where should I put a new knowledge item? Choose the folder that matches the primary use case. If it supports multiple modules, choose the main category and add related links. ## Q: Do I need metadata? Yes. Metadata makes the item easier to search, review, and use with agents later. ## Q: Can I submit draft content? Yes. Mark `status: draft` in the frontmatter.

title: NSEAP Knowledge Base Intro
searchText: kb-overview-001 NSEAP Knowledge Base Intro overview student teacher builder agent nseap knowledge-base elite20 NSEAP Knowledge Base Intro Purpose Content When To Use Agent Notes # NSEAP Knowledge Base Intro ## Purpose Explain the role of the Knowledge Base in the Elite20 Builder Program. ## Content The Knowledge Base is the shared memory layer for the NSEAP AI Learning Operating System. It stores course knowledge, challenge guidance, prompts, FAQs, best practices, project cases, and agent context in a consistent format. ## When To Use Use this entry when introducing why the Knowledge Team exists and how it supports other teams. ## Agent Notes Retrieve this entry when a user asks what the Knowledge Base is, why it matters, or how it supports the project.

title: Sample AI Study Assistant Project
searchText: kb-project-001 Sample AI Study Assistant Project project student teacher builder agent project-case ai-assistant learning-support 项目 项目案例 学习助手 AI 学习助手 project case ai-study-assistant study-planning learning-reflection define-user-situation design-assistant-prompt evaluate-learning-support kb-challenge-001 kb-prompt-001 Sample AI Study Assistant Project Purpose Situation Ontology Workflow Skills Solution Deliverables Evaluation Knowledge Growth Reuse Notes Agent Notes # Sample AI Study Assistant Project ## Purpose Show what a simple project case entry should include. ## Situation Students need a lightweight assistant that helps plan study sessions and review progress. ## Ontology Key concepts include learner, study goal, available time, difficulty, study plan, progress review, and reflection prompt. ## Workflow ```text Collect learning goal -> collect time and difficulty -> generate study plan -> ask reflection question -> review usefulness -> improve assistant prompt ``` ## Skills This project uses situation modeling, assistant prompt design, structured conversation design, and output evaluation. ## Solution Create an AI assistant that asks for the learning goal, time available, and current difficulty, then generates a study plan and reflection prompt. ## Deliverables - Assistant prompt. - Example conversation. - Short demo. - Reflection notes. ## Evaluation Evaluate whether the assistant produces a useful plan, asks relevant questions, and helps the learner reflect. ## Knowledge Growth This project can add reusable assets back into the Knowledge Cognitive Cell: - A study-planning prompt. - A project case. - FAQ entries about learning with AI. - Skill nodes for study planning and reflection. ## Reuse Notes This case can be reused as a starter example for the first AI assistant challenge. ## Agent Notes Retrieve this entry when a student or builder needs a simple example of an AI assistant project.

title: Coding Coach Prompt
searchText: kb-prompt-001 Coding Coach Prompt prompt student agent coding-coach debugging learning 提示词 编程教练 代码调试 Coding Coach debugging coaching debugging guided-learning ask-clarifying-question explain-code-issue create-practice-task kb-course-001 kb-challenge-001 Coding Coach Prompt Purpose Prompt Input Needed Expected Output Graph Notes Agent Notes # Coding Coach Prompt ## Purpose Help a student debug code while still learning the underlying concept. ## Prompt You are my coding coach. Help me understand the problem before giving the final answer. Ask one clarifying question if needed. Then explain the issue, show the smallest useful fix, and give me one practice task to confirm I understand. ## Input Needed - Code snippet. - Error message or unexpected behavior. - What the student already tried. ## Expected Output - Diagnosis. - Explanation. - Minimal fix. - Practice task. ## Graph Notes This entry can become a future `Prompt` node that supports coding challenges and course units. ## Agent Notes Use this prompt pattern when the learner needs coaching rather than direct answer dumping.

title: Overview
searchText: Overview # Overview This folder explains what the knowledge base is, who it serves, and how it connects to other NSEAP modules.

searchText: Course Knowledge # Course Knowledge This folder stores course-level knowledge such as weekly overviews, learning objectives, teacher notes, and learning paths.

searchText: Challenge Knowledge # Challenge Knowledge This folder stores challenge descriptions, goals, steps, deliverables, rubrics, and common mistakes.

searchText: Prompt Library # Prompt Library This folder stores reusable prompts for learning, building, reviewing, and agent operation.

searchText: Project Cases # Project Cases This folder stores project examples, case studies, and reusable references.

searchText: Agent Knowledge # Agent Knowledge This folder stores agent roles, context requirements, instructions, and knowledge-use patterns.

title: Knowledge Base
searchText: Knowledge Base Folders # Knowledge Base This folder stores reusable knowledge entries for the NSEAP AI Learning Operating System. Use the matching folder and template for each new item. ## Folders - `00-overview`: system-level explanations. - `01-course`: course knowledge. - `02-challenges`: challenge knowledge. - `03-prompts`: reusable prompts. - `04-faq`: common questions. - `05-best-practices`: best practices and operating methods. - `06-projects`: project cases and examples. - `07-agents`: agent roles, context, and usage notes.
